item.title: Re: Image Attribution
item.content:encoded:
If you don't mind me suggesting the replacements, how about these? I think these are close enough to the first images, and also under proper Creative Commons.

Crow: One, Two, Three.

Aurora: One, Two, Three.

item.title: Re: Image Attribution
item.content:encoded: Pexels does not fall under Creative Commons, but instead uses their own license which is incompatible with the site's license. The images have been removed due to this reason.

item.title: Image Attribution
item.content:encoded:
Both new images are cropped from free use pictures on Pexels.com; the pages specify that no attribution is required.

https://www.pexels.com/photo/grayscale-photography-of-person-holding-stick-with-two-birds-perching-on-it-2395279/

https://www.pexels.com/photo/northern-lights-during-night-time-3536278/

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Noting that as non-commercial licenses are not compliant with CC BY-SA 3.0, the second image has also been taken down.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: As the first image has not been sourced and could not be sourced, it is to be assumed that it's non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) and has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This article has been updated to improve its clarity and fluidity. Also, new media! Yay.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The second image is borrowed from:

Original Source: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Aurora_australis_panorama.jpg
Creator: Fir0002/Flagstaffotos

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This may be nitpicky, but birds don't have anuses. They have a cloaca, which serves as their genitalia, urethra, and anus (so saying it doesn't have genitalia or an anus is anatomically incorrect, as birds don't really have those in the first place).

item.title: Loved it but..
item.content:encoded: ..I don't know why but the fact that it constantly has to be immolated at 1500c bugged me, the cost of resources would definitely catch the counsels eye ( seeing as how they were antsy about a dog ordering lobster). Thumbs up for scp though and definitely want more 1428-2

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The expungement of the object in the statue's hand not only doesn't add anything to this article, it actively hurts it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, also, there's more to the story in SCP-2995.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am seriously loving this. I read these in reverse order (2814 first). Waitaminit…1428/2814…I sense a pattern in the force.

Maniacal ramblings...
...oh the ramifications.

I am surmising that the statue is either Xihe or Houyi, if you're using the rest of the myths. I'm guessing (pure speculation) that the item in the third hand is a portion of a second 1428-1. Hmm, two suns loose on the planet at once. Better than ten, I suppose. But to finish this ramble, the [DATA EXPUNGED], if allowed to heal, will contact the sun and cause an event similar to 2170 BCE (which I would think this temple was built around the time of). I'm taking it from the "little sun" that the one inhabiting ours is currently passive/inactive; the contact should wake it up/make it go active. Then we sizzle.

The mask (SCP-2814) is a device to heat up the sun and keep it pacified, just like Contingency Fusang-A being used to pacify 1428-1.


Nice fragging keter, my good man. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I give it a +1. I love most of the Keter classes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Started off quite well with the radioactive crow, but then the temple stuff seemed to me as trying too hard to make it a bigger thing than it was and to make it creepy. In fact, I feel like the temple part would work better as its own entry.

I am also not a fan of the amount of redaction and expungement. It feels like it's been used unnecessarily at various points just to make it more mysterious.

I am also not sure what we're supposed to get from the "emergency containment review" (or why it was even recorded in the first place since it reads more like a casual conversation than an official meeting), nor why Dr. Albert seems so agitated. There is nothing about what he describes that indicates any kind of threat, and although it's true that the SCP personnel has seen some shit, it still seems odd that they'd believe an ancient prophecy without solid evidence backing it up.

In conclusion, neutral vote for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ALL HAIL THE SUN DUCK.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: judging by comments im happy i got to this after edits. it seems pretty good now

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The story of this article is finally continued in SCP-2814.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm afraid this Keter level SCP will inevitably lead to an XK class scenario either way. If the doctor is true, containing this will only delay the death of all life on the planet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've always wondered how april fools was managed over here. I am not disappointed. That crow is great.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So stylish and…. informative on protecting one's eye's against ultraviolet radiation and bright lights!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the fashion crow

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was really playing with fire (haha) with that expungement.

infant

head

I was hoping that people would come to conclusions like these (mainly the latter), but I wanted them to be only 90% sure that it was a head. I wanted them to be 10% sure that it was something distinctly more sinister…though, whoever said it was a human infant or head?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is pretty good, but I'm not happy with the expungement. Right now I'm imagining this statue holding a human infant, and I know that this is probably wrong. Maybe it's a head. Who knows. Point is, that I think a little too much has been left to my imagination. But it's a well written article all the same.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you, Murklins, for your interest in helping my article (and for the upvote), but I prefer to use the spelling "amnesic" of the three outlined in the FAQ.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The sources (no, it is not wikipedia) I checked before writing this say that electron volts, while being a unit of energy, are used to measure momentum in gamma particles. I need to state the momentum to tell us how far the particles will travel in air.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Tungsten cage

If the cage is merely made from the element tungsten then it doesn't need to be capitalized.

SCP-1428-1 is to be constantly incinerated at a temperature of 1,500 degrees Centigrade. Under no circumstances are any personnel to enter the containment chamber or make direct physical contact with SCP-1428-1.

Do people smart enough to work for the Foundation really have to be explicitly told not to stick their hand into an active 1,500 C incinerator?

There is no clear consistency in the charge of the radiation

previously recorded charges have

That should be "intensity", not charge.

with a momentum of 20 MeV.

MeV is a unit of energy, not momentum. And anyways, in this context why would anyone care about how much momentum all of the gamma radiation photons have?

item.title: Re: this is p bad
item.content:encoded:
The bird is an aerial source of gamma radiation that turns into a Cobalt Bomb every few months, I wouldn't say that's Euclid. Even without the implications of the temple it's still a huge threat. Also the containment has to be very specific, and any accidental harm to the bird will cause a containment breach. I respect your thoughts, but the way I see this thing, it's too dangerous and volatile to be considered Euclid.

I know it's a little hazy regarding [REDACTED] and [DATA EXPUNGED], sorry about that. [DATA EXPUNGED] is the thing that's regenerating in the statue of [REDACTED]'s hand.

item.title: Re: this is p bad
item.content:encoded:
It's certainly not safe, but the classification of Keter still seems undeserved to me. The bird is no more dangerous than a large bomb yes? The only justification for Keter is the carvings at the temple and a lot of speculation on what happens if [REDACTED] finishes regenerating.

I recommend it be reclassified as Euclid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm. I still would say that the dialogue is quite wooden, notably, 'where [REDACTED] came from'. Also, I am not sure if this was intentional or not, but the two [REDACTED]s are quite awkward to read when the sentence is awkward enough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I see. Never heard of it, though I am familiar with the ten suns and crows.
Maybe you don't need to reveal it. Didn't do much for me. You could consider just mentioning the burned [REDACTED] wanting the immolation, and it would have the same effect, without having the sentence sounding awkward, like that doctor is some sort of prophet. I think it would be much better that way, but that's just me.

item.title: Re: this is p bad
item.content:encoded:
The Foundation, strictly speaking, is not aware of why the thing hasn't healed fully (it does not heal over time, it only regenerates when people get burned in front of it; the reason it has regenerated since coming into Foundation custody is because of D-Class burnt during testing and Fusang-A). I would imagine that the cult was probably wiped out before they could burn enough people to heal it fully.

As it says in the article, the crow was not found at the temple but within a hundred kilometers of it, and the Foundation does not know how long the crow has been there. Also, it is very far from Safe. Even without the threat of [REDACTED], it's still a radioactive bird that releases gamma ray bursts every few months.

item.title: Re: this is p bad
item.content:encoded:
There are many scenarios that would explain this, and not being the author I can't say which was intended. So I'll just throw out the first couple off the top of my head.

1)The healing is accelerating. In the first thousand years, it only healed 5%. Second thousand got it up to 20%. Now that it's over 2/3 regenerated, it's really starting to pick up steam.

2)The crow is new. The temple, and presumably the object, is X thousand years old. But that doesn't mean the deathcrow has been here the whole time. Maybe (redacted) had to save it's wounded strength for millenia in order to summon it up, with the explicit intent of forcing us into burnt offerings and starting it's healign process.

3)The monks new a better ritual. People who don't fit in haven't fared well in China in recent decades, I'm thinking the human-sacrificing fire cult would be a prime target for re-education, or something a bit more permanent. It's possible the original monks new a way to calm the crow down that didn't feed the thingy. But by the time anyone knew that it was important, they weren't around to talk to anymore. The Foundation is running a stop-gap based on damaged murals and crumbling scrolls, and it isn't working nearly as well.

item.title: this is p bad
item.content:encoded:
f this thing bird been out there for thousands of years just hanging out shouldn't the ooh scary [redacted] object have regenerated by now? probably? so why hasn't it? was it safe in the temple where it was found? ok, then take it out of the crappy containment where it is now (since it's obviously not working) and throw it back in the temple, surround it with guards, and mark it safe.

this article just strikes me as ill-conceived and grimdark for the sake of grimdark.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I made a slight edit to Dr. Albert's dialogue.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes, it's a three-legged crow that lives in the sun. To be fair, I've always been iffy myself on that segment of dialogue, I just wasn't sure how to fix it while leaving the content revealed intact.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I believe this should help.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dr. Albert: It will call out to the sun, where [REDACTED] and SCP-1428-1 came from, and where [REDACTED] returned to when-

Broke the immersion for me, a bit unrealistic for people to actually say, especially when said people are scientists and doctors.
Also, is Jinwu a real deity? Judging from the comments it is, but I have never heard of it before, nor can I find it on Google.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The cage is to make sure that the drones don't accidentally hurt the bird during repair; I chose Tungsten because it has a very high melting point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just a quick clarification, "biological" in the tags is referring to the burnt thing in the statue's hand, not the bird.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ah yes, the Yangwu. I knew it as soon as it said "tripedal".
Well, I've always wanted to see this done, and I do like it enough to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't particularly dislike it but I've decided to withhold on voting on SCP's with a twist ending for the foreseeable future. Every SCP I read now I scroll to the bottom to see if there's a collapsible and if there is I'm already thinking "meh".
e. Also, using really specific percentages for things that are hard to measure always bugs me, I think people tend to do that in articles to make things look ultra sciency.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've noticed that this article used the phrase "appears to" way too many times, so I cut some out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fair enough. I'll think about how to clean that up a little.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Still a bit unclear how they figured that out in the first place. I might place that earlier or clearer, as right now, the connection between the two is tenuous. Still, a good hook.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They didn't start burning people until they found texts at the temple/shrine that said it would assuage the bird's active states; that's what I mean by "corroborated with" in the last paragraph.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's well done, but I'm unclear on why the Foundation started incinerating D-class when SCP-1428-1 got all uppity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've made a very slight tweak to the containment review section.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like the expungements in this. I think enough is left unexpunged to make them work. Admittedly, the bird by itself isn't all that interesting, and getting to the statue quicker, or at least mentioning it sooner, might be a good idea. I would also recommend reworking the interview log at the end. I kind of thought that the expunged thing was actually the focus, but having someone just up and know what it's going to do ruins the effect a bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now this is working for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Your new edits tie it together more, and I am now quite interested. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've added a collapsible section, I have no idea whether this will work, so, just take a look and tell me what you think.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I almost like this. I'm just struggling to see the connection between -1 and -2.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just added a little more material.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm still thinking on how to tie that third object in with Jinwu's state cycle and the whole human immolation thing; I'll post here if I add something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I do know what's in its hand, and I'm still thinking about how to make that section more interesting. The passive state still generates enough gamma radiation to kill people, but it has to be much closer than an active state (due to the electron Volt/momentum difference). However, it's a mobile, flying source of lethal gamma radiation even when it's not active, making it too dangerous to leave alone even while passive.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, you get points in my book for not making the black corvid a common crow or raven.

That being said, it's not bad, but it doesn't really do anything for me, either. Four things:

1. I think that you focus too much on 1428-1 and not enough on 1428-2.

2. Is there any point to the "passive state"?

3. To me, they seem too disjointed, feeling almost like two related SCPs rather than two parts of the same SCP.

4. What could 1428-2 possibly be holding in its third hand that would warrant expungement? This omission doesn't add to the creepy/horror/mystery value for me, it just really makes me think that you don't know yourself what's in its hand.

No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this. It shouldn't get deleted. Good work man.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This isn't quite enough to upvote, but not downvoting it either. I see you're hinting at something with the fixation on Site-108, but I have no bloody idea what.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: S'just Jinwu/Yatagarasu as an SCP. Nothing really blows my skirt up here.
